mnemonics.title: F.I.R.S.T Functions
mnemonics.content: Function Input Returns Something True: F(unction) I(nput) R(eturns) S(omething) T(ype) - Remembers the key components: name, parameters, return type

mnemonics.title: C.A.S.T. Away Your Type Worries
mnemonics.content: C(onst). A(static). S(dynamic). T(reinterpret) - The four C++ casts: const_cast, static_cast, dynamic_cast, reinterpret_cast

mnemonics.title: P.A.R.A. Parameters
mnemonics.content: Pass Arguments, Receive Answer - P(ass) A(rguments) R(eceive) A(nswer) - Remember function parameters work by passing arguments and receiving results

mnemonics.title: I.S.A. - Int Smaller Automatically
mnemonics.content: Implicit conversion: Int Smaller Automatically converts to larger types during arithmetic to prevent data loss

mnemonics.title: R.E.C.U.R.S.E. Recursion
mnemonics.content: Remember Every Call Unto Recursive State Exists - Base case stops the recursion, recursive call continues until base case reached

acronyms.expansion: Variable With No Information or Type
acronyms.usage: Used when function returns nothing or pointer has no specific type
Compile-time casts between related types (int to float, void* to int*, base to derived with known types)
Safe downcasting in inheritance hierarchy; returns nullptr on failure for pointers
Add or remove const/volatile qualifiers; only way to modify const object in C++
Reinterpret bits of one type as another; low-level, machine-dependent, use sparingly
Automatically safe; derived to base conversion in inheritance
Requires explicit cast; unsafe without dynamic_cast check

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a factory with an INPUT CONVEYOR BELT (parameters), a PROCESSING MACHINE (function body), and an OUTPUT CONVEYOR BELT (return value). The machine takes raw materials, processes them, and delivers finished products.

Think of types as different languages: int speaks 'integer', float speaks 'decimal'. Casting is like a translator converting one language to another. Implicit cast = fluent translator (automatic), Explicit cast = dictionary lookup (manual static_cast).

Visualize a STACK OF PLATES: each function call adds a plate (stack frame) on top. Parameters and local variables live on that plate. When function ends, plate is removed (popped). Last plate added = first removed (LIFO).
Picture a LADDER: char → int → long → float → double. Moving UP the ladder (to larger type) is safe and automatic. Moving DOWN requires explicit cast and may lose data.
REFERENCE = Nickname (alias), POINTER = Street Address. You can change what a pointer points to (new address), but reference always refers to original variable (cannot be reassigned).

analogies.analogy: RESTAURANT MENU: Same dish name (function name) but different preparations (parameters). 'Chicken curry' can be mild, spicy, or with extra vegetables - same name, different ingredients (arguments), different taste (behavior).
HOTEL ROOM with standard amenities: Every room comes with bed, TV, AC by default. You can request extra items (custom arguments). If you don't specify, defaults are used automatically.
STATIC = Looking at a photo ID to verify (compile-time check). DYNAMIC = Actually talking to the person to verify their identity (runtime check with RTTI).
PASS BY VALUE = Sending a PHOTOCOPY of a document (original stays safe). PASS BY REFERENCE = Giving someone the ORIGINAL document (they can modify it directly).

RUSSIAN NESTING DOLLS: Each doll contains a smaller version of itself. To reach the smallest, you must open each outer doll. The smallest doll (base case) stops the opening. Then dolls close back up in reverse order (returning).

READ-ONLY HOTEL KEY CARD: Initially you have a key that cannot modify room settings (const). But you need to temporarily adjust thermostat. const_cast is like getting a temporary admin key to make that one change.

MORSE CODE TRANSLATION: Converting English text to Morse code doesn't change the meaning, just the representation. reinterpret_cast converts data representation without changing underlying bits - just like changing alphabet to dots and dashes.
